evidence_quotes.quote: they aimed to ban GTA V for "violence against women", like women are the only thing that can be receive violence

Detroit Become Human for 'violence against a woman', that woman is Kara, literally one of the main protagonists, an Android also, and that was literally the plot, her story, the things she does in the game is because of that

paragraphs.text: Please, you should consider giving a look and a consideration to the Stop Killing Games movement. Also there's been a recent problem with payment processor (Mastercard, Visa and PayPal) choosing what user can or cannot buy (They have threatened Steam and itch.io recently to not be their payment processor anymore if they didn't listen to their demands, so they had to comply) because a small group of femminists and who knows who (called Collective Shouts) started attacking trough phone calls and emails those payment processor because they had to follow their personal agenda (They say they want to protect children and women violence, but they defended publicly a movie called "Cuties" that is know for having exploited children, and also they aimed to ban GTA V for "violence against women", like women are the only thing that can be receive violence, and Detroit Become Human for "violence against a woman", that woman is Kara, literally one of the main protagonists, an Android also, and that was literally the plot, her story, the things she does in the game is because of that, it's like trying to ban John Wick movies because of his dog), they do not aim to protect them, and after all it's all fiction, people can buy whatever they want if it's legal, payment processor should just process their payment, without asking questions (That's how they earn their money afterall). I hope you consider looking into these things, because we love anonimity, privacy, a freedom to buy and get what we choose to buy (and also keep it).
